$comment: SOURCE OF TRUTH for tela's authorable block palette. Drives BOTH the editor slash menu (frontend/src/components/app/milkdown-slash.tsx) AND the agent-facing MCP authoring guide (backend/internal/api/mcp_authoring.go, via the generated backend/internal/api/blocks_gen.json). Edit here only; run `make blocks-gen` to regenerate the backend copy. A renderer plugin with no entry here is invisible to agents AND missing from the slash palette — the blocks gate fails CI if a milkdown-* feature plugin has no entry. Fields: id (stable, joins to the slash `run` fn), label/hint/keywords (slash menu), category (guide grouping), slash (appears in the palette), agent (appears in the agent guide), syntax (exact round-trip markdown), when (one-line agent guidance), note (optional caveat).

blocks.label: To-do list
blocks.when: A checklist of toggleable items.

blocks.label: Pull quote
blocks.when: Feature a quotation or epigraph, set apart from body text.

blocks.label: Callout
blocks.when: Set off an aside, tip, or warning so it stands out from body text.
blocks.note: Type is one of NOTE, TIP, IMPORTANT, WARNING, CAUTION.

blocks.label: Collapsible
blocks.when: Hide long or secondary content behind a toggle.
blocks.note: Blank lines around the body are required. Add `open` to <details> to start expanded.

blocks.label: Tabs
blocks.syntax:
:::tabs
### First tab
Content for the first tab.

### Second tab
Content for the second tab.
:::
blocks.when: Show alternative content in the same space (e.g. per-language examples).
blocks.note: Each ### heading inside the block becomes a tab label.

blocks.label: Kanban board
blocks.syntax:
:::kanban
### To do
- [ ] A card

### In progress
- [ ] Another card
:::
blocks.when: A board of columns holding draggable cards.
blocks.note: Each ### heading is a column; bullet items are its cards.

blocks.label: Stat grid
blocks.when: Show a row of headline numbers / KPIs, each with a label and a value.
blocks.note: Each ### heading is a tile label; the text below is its value. A ↑ or ↓ (or +/-) in the value tints the tile green/red automatically.

blocks.label: Timeline
blocks.syntax:
:::timeline
- **2026-01-15** v1.0 shipped — first stable release
- **2026-03-01** v1.1 — search + exports
:::
blocks.when: Show a chronological sequence of dated events (roadmap, release history).
blocks.note: Each bullet is an event; lead with a bold date. Put them in chronological order yourself.

blocks.label: Calendar
blocks.when: Lay out dated, all-day events on a single month grid (schedules, launch plans).
blocks.note: Set month=YYYY-MM on the directive. Each bullet is `YYYY-MM-DD Title` inside that month. One month per block; all-day only.

blocks.label: Embed
blocks.hint: YouTube, Vimeo, Loom, or a link
blocks.when: Embed a video or external page inline.
blocks.note: YouTube, Vimeo, and Loom render as players; other URLs become link cards.

blocks.label: Mermaid diagram
blocks.when: Flowcharts, sequence/state/ER diagrams, pie charts — anything Mermaid supports.

blocks.label: Excalidraw
blocks.when: Hand-drawn diagram / whiteboard.
blocks.note: Authored visually in the editor — not meant to be hand-written by agents.

blocks.label: Image
blocks.when: Embed an image.
blocks.note: In the editor, drag-drop or paste uploads the file automatically.

blocks.label: Code block
blocks.when: Syntax-highlighted code with a copy button.
blocks.note: Put the language right after the opening fence.

blocks.label: Equation
blocks.when: Display a standalone mathematical formula (KaTeX/LaTeX).

blocks.label: Inline math
blocks.when: Inline LaTeX within a sentence.
blocks.note: No space directly inside the $…$ delimiters.

blocks.label: Table
blocks.when: Tabular data (GitHub-flavored markdown).

blocks.label: Highlight
blocks.when: Mark a span as if with a highlighter pen.

blocks.label: Wikilink
blocks.when: Link to another page in the same space by title; records a backlink.
blocks.note: Resolved by title within the space. tela://page/{id} also works as a link target.

blocks.label: Footnote
blocks.syntax:
A claim that needs a source.[^1]

[^1]: The supporting note.
blocks.when: Add a footnote; the reader renders an auto-numbered back-reference.
